rationale: The citing paper’s design and findings explicitly hinge on utilitarian versus non-utilitarian purchasing (e.g., a follow-up experiment on recognized utilitarian products), while the cited paper is a core study on consumer choice between hedonic and utilitarian goods and related reference-state mechanisms. That makes the cited work a plausible conceptual source rather than an incidental mention. With an exact DOI match, this pair should be retained for full relation judgment.
